title: VERINOX AEGISLOOP: Sovereign Evidence-Closed Recovery Fabric for Cyber-Physical Systems
description:
Problem. Autonomous recovery actions in cyber-physical systems can execute without binding independent pre-evidence, predicted dual counterfactual outcome envelopes, one-use actuation authority, multi-horizon measured post-evidence, and revocable learning lineage into one admissible recovery capsule.

Gap. Watchdogs, failover, digital twins, and many runtime-assurance schemes provide pieces of the puzzle but rarely close a full Evidence-Closed Recovery Loop (ECRL) that refuses learning when independence, envelopes, bonds, or measured closure disagree.

Proposed solution. VERINOX AEGISLOOP defines ECRL: claim-scoped independent pre-evidence, dual counterfactual envelopes, one-use actuation bonds, multi-horizon post-evidence, digest-bound capsule closure, and a learning gate with fail-closed verdicts (CLOSURE_PASS, ENVELOPE_VIOLATION, EVIDENCE_INDEPENDENT_FAIL, BOND_REUSED, LEARNING_REVOKED).

Key evidence (v3.0.0). Importable library from verinox_aegisloop import VerinoxEngine; Reality Gate PASS 8/8; benchmarks; mutation testing ≥90%; alternative-implementation replication; numeric coolant-recovery real-world scenario; stress tests to 2000 pre-evidence pairs. Companion v2.0 blueprint content is preserved (NO SHRINK).

Honest limits. Not IEC 61508 / ISO 26262 certified, not a patent grant, not production CPS control, not peer reviewed, and not independently third-party replicated. Concept DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21364737. Released for evaluation, citation, and attempted falsification. PDF regeneration may still be required.
notes: Independent Research Publication No. 5. v3.0.0. Concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.21364737. PDF REGENERATION NEEDED. Not peer reviewed. Not certified functional safety. Generative AI tools assisted preparation. Author Agim Haxhijaha (ORCID 0009-0002-3234-7765) remains responsible for all claims, omissions, and release decisions.
